title: Goniophlebium argutum
intro: Goniophlebium argutum, also known as the "sharp-leafed Goniophlebium," is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Asian medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Goniophlebium argutum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Goniophlebium argutum is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or powder, or used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and formulas for treating various health conditions.
taxonomy: Goniophlebium argutum is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. The Polypodiaceae family is a large and diverse group of ferns, commonly known as the polypody ferns. This family is part of the larger division Polypodopsida, which also includes other families of ferns. The classification of Goniophlebium argutum further breaks down to the genus Goniophlebium and the species level, indicating its unique characteristics within the genus. The classification helps identify and distinguish the species from other related ferns.
